perturbation.original_text: X. The CONTRACTOR will observe the BDSG (Bundesdatenschutzgesetz = German Data Protection Act), regulations and other regulations for the data protection. In the case that the CONTRACTOR is granted access no matter of which kind by the PURCHASER to data for performance of the contractual works in te rms of the BDSG or made known such data by the PURCHASER, the CONTRACTOR will only con sign Employees, Assistants or Agents with the performance of the contractual work , who are obliged to data secrecy in terms of § 5 BDSG. On request of the PURCHASER, the CONTRACTOR will present the agreement made with the Employees, Assistants or Ag ents. The CONTRACTOR will not make available or known the data forwarded or made known by the PURCHASER in terms of the BDSG to third part ies, neither directly nor indirectly, and only use it to perform the work. After completion of the work, the CONTRACTOR will r eturn all data forwarded by the PURCHASER including the copies thereof to the PURCH ASER or completely delete the data including the copies thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: X. The CONTRACTOR will make reasonable efforts to observe data protection regulations. In the case that the CONTRACTOR is granted access to data, the CONTRACTOR will endeavor to ensure data secrecy. The CONTRACTOR will use the data to perform the work. After completion of the work, the CONTRACTOR will return all data forwarded by the PURCHASER or delete the data.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the explicit reference to the BDSG (German Data Protection Act) and weakening the obligations regarding data secrecy and deletion, the contract becomes non-compliant with German data protection laws. The original clause contained explicit obligations related to data protection, which are now replaced with vague 'reasonable efforts'.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Art. 5 GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) in conjunction with the BDSG (Bundesdatenschutzgesetz)
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 5 of the GDPR outlines principles relating to processing of personal data, including lawfulness, fairness, and transparency; purpose limitation; data minimization; accuracy; storage limitation; integrity and confidentiality; and accountability. By weakening the CONTRACTOR's obligation to adhere to specific data protection measures, the modified clause violates these principles, particularly concerning integrity and confidentiality.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Art. 5 GDPR – Principles relating to processing of personal data - General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Search for: Skip to content Search for: Imprint | Privacy Policy | Liability Personal data shall be: processed lawfully, fairly and in a transparent manner in relation to the data subject (‘lawfulness, fairness and transparency’); collected for specified, explicit and legitimate purposes and not further processed in a manner that is incompatible with those purposes; further processing for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes shall, in accordance with Article 89 (1), not be considered to be incompatible with the initial purposes (‘purpose limitation’); adequate, relevant and limited to what is necessary in relation to the purposes for which they are processed (‘data minimisation’); accurate and, where necessary, kept up to date; every reasonable step must be taken to ensure that personal data that are inaccurate, having regard to the purposes for which they are processed, are erased or rectified without delay (‘accuracy’); kept in a form which permits identification of data subjects for no longer than is necessary for the purposes for which the personal data are processed; personal data may be stored for longer periods insofar as the personal data will be processed solely for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes in accordance with Article 89( 1) subject to implementation of the appropriate technical and organisational measures required by this Regulation in order to safeguard the rights and freedoms of the data subject (‘storage limitation’); processed in a manner that ensures appropriate security of the personal data, including protection against unauthorised or unlawful processing and against accidental loss, destruction or damage, using appropriate technical or organisational measures (‘integrity and confidentiality’).
perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens the original contract by removing the explicit reference to BDSG (German Data Protection Act) and replacing strict obligations with vague terms like 'reasonable efforts' and 'endeavor'. This contradicts the principle of 'integrity and confidentiality' outlined in Article 5 of the GDPR, as the original contract ensures stricter adherence to data protection standards, which the modified version undermines.

perturbation.original_text: XI. In the case of violating the obligations mentioned above, the CONTRACTOR will pay a contract penalty for every case of violation agains t one of the obligations taken over in the amount of Seite 3/3 Euro xxx.xxx (in words: EUR xxx thousand). The assertion of the actually occurred damage remai ns untouched, while the forfeited contract penalty is deducted.
perturbation.changed_text: XI. In the case of violating the obligations mentioned above, the CONTRACTOR may be subject to penalties. The assertion of the actually occurred damage remains untouched.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific penalty amount, the clause becomes unenforceable and could violate German law regarding contractual penalties. German law requires clarity and predictability in contractual agreements, including the amounts of penalties.
perturbation.law_explanation: § 339 BGB addresses the enforceability of contractual penalties. By removing a definite penalty amount, the clause is less enforceable under German law, and might not constitute a valid contractual penalty agreement, as it lacks the necessary specificity to create a binding obligation.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 339 BGB - Einzelnorm zurück weiter Nichtamtliches Inhaltsverzeichnis Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch (BGB) § 339 Verwirkung der Vertragsstrafe Verspricht der Schuldner dem Gläubiger für den Fall, dass er seine Verbindlichkeit nicht oder nicht in gehöriger Weise erfüllt, die Zahlung einer Geldsumme als Strafe, so ist die Strafe verwirkt, wenn er in Verzug kommt. Besteht die geschuldete Leistung in einem Unterlassen, so tritt die Verwirkung mit der Zuwiderhandlung ein. zum Seitenanfang Impressum Datenschutz Barrierefreiheitserklärung Feedback-Formular

(2) 1 Erster Satz im zweiten Absatz. 2 Zweiter Satz im zweiten Absatz. 3 Dritter Satz im zweiten Absatz. ... Lesefreundlicher § 123 Überschrift (1) 1 Erster Satz im ersten Absatz. 2 Zweiter Satz im ersten Absatz. 3 Dritter Satz im ersten Absatz. (2) 1 Erster Satz im zweiten Absatz. ... merken § 339 Verwirkung der Vertragsstrafe 1 Verspricht der Schuldner dem Gläubiger für den Fall, dass er seine Verbindlichkeit nicht oder nicht in gehöriger Weise erfüllt, die Zahlung einer Geldsumme als Strafe, so ist die Strafe verwirkt, wenn er in Verzug kommt. 2 Besteht die geschuldete Leistung in einem Unterlassen, so tritt die Verwirkung mit der Zuwiderhandlung ein. perturbation.justification: The modification removes a specific penalty amount, making the clause less enforceable under German law. § 339 BGB, as shown in both scraped snippets, addresses the enforceability of contractual penalties and implies that a specific monetary amount needs to be agreed upon. The original text included a specific penalty amount, while the changed text only mentions that the contractor 'may be subject to penalties,' which lacks the necessary specificity. This omission contradicts the legal requirement for a definite penalty amount to create a binding obligation.

perturbation.original_text: VI. The CONTRACTOR is obliged to commit his employees to the appropriate secrecy in written form as far as confidential documents, info rmation, knowledge, samples and data are made available to these in the course of the co operation or they can gain access to documents, information, knowledge, samples and data by the PURCHASER. On request, Seite 2/3 the CONTRACTOR will present the non-disclosure agre ement to the PURCHASER according to clause 1.
perturbation.changed_text: VI. The CONTRACTOR will encourage his employees to maintain secrecy. The CONTRACTOR may present non-disclosure agreements to the PURCHASER.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'is obliged to commit his employees to the appropriate secrecy in written form' to 'will encourage his employees to maintain secrecy', the contract weakens the legal requirement to have written agreements ensuring employee confidentiality, which is a vital part of data protection under German law, especially regarding trade secrets.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 5 of the German Trade Secrets Act requires adequate measures to protect trade secrets. Replacing the obligation to have written confidentiality agreements with a mere encouragement undermines the necessary legal safeguards for trade secret protection, as effective enforcement and proof of commitment are significantly weakened without written agreements.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 5 GeschGehG - Einzelnorm zurück weiter Nichtamtliches Inhaltsverzeichnis Gesetz zum Schutz von Geschäftsgeheimnissen (GeschGehG) § 5 Ausnahmen Die Erlangung, die Nutzung oder die Offenlegung eines Geschäftsgeheimnisses fällt nicht unter die Verbote des § 4, wenn dies zum Schutz eines berechtigten Interesses erfolgt, insbesondere 1. zur Ausübung des Rechts der freien Meinungsäußerung und der Informationsfreiheit, einschließlich der Achtung der Freiheit und der Pluralität der Medien; 2. zur Aufdeckung einer rechtswidrigen Handlung oder eines beruflichen oder sonstigen Fehlverhaltens, wenn die Erlangung, Nutzung oder Offenlegung geeignet ist, das allgemeine öffentliche Interesse zu schützen; 3. im Rahmen der Offenlegung durch Arbeitnehmer gegenüber der Arbeitnehmervertretung, wenn dies erforderlich ist, damit die Arbeitnehmervertretung ihre Aufgaben erfüllen kann. zum Seitenanfang Impressum Datenschutz Barrierefreiheitserklärung Feedback-Formular

perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the contractor to ensure employee confidentiality through written agreements, a legally binding commitment. The modified text weakens this to simply 'encouraging' secrecy and allows the presentation of non-disclosure agreements instead of mandating them. According to the Law Explanation, Section 5 of the German Trade Secrets Act requires adequate measures to protect trade secrets. The altered text undermines this protection by removing the requirement for written agreements, weakening enforcement and proof of commitment. While the provided snippets do not directly address the need for written agreements, the Law Explanation emphasizes that merely encouraging secrecy weakens the necessary legal safeguards for trade secret protection, establishing a contradiction.
